topic: Developing Sentences and Proofreading for Impact
title: Crafting Powerful Sentences and Polishing Your Writing

questions.question: What does "adverbial phrase" mean?
options: A group of words that tells you more about the verb, such as where, when, or how something happened.
A sentence that contains one main clause (a subject and a verb).
The repetition of the same initial consonant sound in words close together.
To read text and mark any errors.
questions.correct_answer: A group of words that tells you more about the verb, such as where, when, or how something happened.
questions.explanation: "adverbial phrase" means: A group of words that tells you more about the verb, such as where, when, or how something happened.

questions.question: What does "alliteration" mean?
options: A sentence that contains one main clause (a subject and a verb).
To read text and mark any errors.
The repetition of the same initial consonant sound in words close together.
A group of words that tells you more about the verb, such as where, when, or how something happened.
questions.correct_answer: The repetition of the same initial consonant sound in words close together.
questions.explanation: "alliteration" means: The repetition of the same initial consonant sound in words close together.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Adverbial phrases add important ______ details to sentences.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Adverbial phrases add important setting details to sentences."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Proofreading is essential to catch and correct mistakes in your writing.
Reading backwards helps in effective proofreading.
Combining adverbial phrases with alliteration enhances descriptive writing.
Adverbial phrases add important setting details to sentences.
questions.correct_answer: Combining adverbial phrases with alliteration enhances descriptive writing.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Combining adverbial phrases with alliteration enhances descriptive writing." is one of the key things to remember.

options: A group of words that tells you more about the verb, such as where, when, or how something happened.
A sentence that contains one main clause (a subject and a verb).
The repetition of the same initial consonant sound in words close together.
To read text and mark any errors.
questions.correct_answer: To read text and mark any errors.
questions.explanation: "proofread" means: To read text and mark any errors.
